Content: *p78458ra01832* do not write in this area    do not write in this area          do not write in this area 18          18  this question is about reaction kinetics.   *(a)  halogenoalkanes are hydrolysed by aqueous alkali to form alcohols. rx   +   oh        roh   +   x      the rate equations for these reactions with two halogenoalkanes are shown. 1-bromobutane rate = k[ch3ch2ch2ch2br][oh ] 2-bromo-2-methylpropane rate = k[(ch3)3cbr]     explain how these rate equations can be used to deduce the different reaction  mechanisms of the reaction of hydroxide ions with these halogenoalkanes.
